task_updates.executor_notes: Every response-loop send now routes through a local `_send_with_retry` helper that retries only `discord.HTTPException` failures with `status >= 500`, using 0.5s then 1.5s backoff and re-raising on exhaustion or any non-5xx response. The outer catch-all now logs with `logger.exception`, sends fixed neutral copy with no raw exception text, and protects that fallback send in its own `try/except` so a second send failure cannot recurse.

Removed the three dead Zapier imports and deleted the Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube call blocks from `finalize_sharing`, leaving the Twitter post, URL announcement, shared-post recording, first-share notification, and duplicate-prevention bookkeeping intact. Static reread of the surrounding block confirms there are no orphaned variables or broken success/failure branches after the removals.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified the only two response-loop send sites now call `_send_with_retry`, which catches only `discord.HTTPException` and re-raises anything with `status < 500` or any third 5xx failure after the 0.5s/1.5s retries. The catch-all no longer interpolates `str(e)` into user-facing text and wraps its neutral fallback send in a separate `try/except`.

Confirmed `sharer.py` now imports only `post_tweet` and `generate_media_title` from `social_poster`, and the Instagram/TikTok/YouTube call blocks are gone. The remaining Twitter path still owns the success/failure branching, URL announcement, DB write, notification, and duplicate-prevention set update without stray references to removed platforms.
